CREATE TABLE cities (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), country VARCHAR(100), postal_code INT);
INSERT INTO cities (id, name, country, postal_code) VALUES (1, 'Amsterdam', 'NL', 1012);
INSERT INTO cities (id, name, country, postal_code) VALUES (2, 'New York', 'US', 10001);
INSERT INTO cities (id, name, country, postal_code) VALUES (3, 'Tokyo', 'JP', 100-0001);
PREPARE my_stmt1 AS SELECT * FROM cities WHERE country = ?;
PREPARE my_stmt2 AS SELECT name FROM cities WHERE postal_code = ? OR country = ?;
PREPARE my_stmt3 AS SELECT c1.name, c2.name FROM cities c1 JOIN cities c2 ON c1.country = c2.country WHERE c1.id != c2.id;
PREPARE my_stmt4 AS SELECT * FROM (SELECT name, country FROM cities) AS subquery WHERE country = ?;
PREPARE my_stmt5 AS SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cities WHERE name ILIKE ?;
PREPARE my_stmt6 AS INSERT INTO cities (id, name, country, postal_code) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?);
PREPARE my_stmt7 AS DELETE FROM cities WHERE id = ?;
PREPARE my_stmt8 AS SELECT DISTINCT country FROM cities WHERE country IS NOT NULL;
PREPARE my_stmt9 AS SELECT id, name FROM cities WHERE id > ? ORDER BY name ASC;
